entry.title: Re: Implement Currency Conversion in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: We don't see TCURR data in a released status in S4HC (public cloud).  This blog post says it is. What gives?

entry.title: SAP GUI及びSAP RouterのWin11 OS対応の確認
entry.content:
There is a plan to update the operating system of the client PC from Win10 to Win11 in the future.

Are the SAP GUI Ver.800 and SAP Router Ver.7.53 patch 422 supported  in Win11 OS?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI及びSAP RouterのWin11 OS対応の確認
entry.content:
SAP Note 26417 - SAP GUI Resources: Hardware and software

SAP Note 66971 - Supported SAP GUI platforms:

  • SAP GUI for Windows 8.00:
    • Windows 10 32-bit (only Education, Pro, and Enterprise editions)
    • Windows 10 64-bit on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T (only Education, Pro, and Enterprise editions)
    • Windows Server 2016 64-bit on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T
    • Windows Server 2019 64-bit on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T
    • Windows 11 on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T (only Education, Pro, and Enterprise editions)
    • Windows Server 2022 on x64 (AMD) and Intel EM64T

 

Regarding the SAProuter query, only below Windows Server versions are supported:

SAP Note 1897597 - SAProuter: Release rollout:

The supported operating systems are as follows:

SAProuter 7.53:

WINDOWS SERVER 2008/X64 64BIT
WINDOWS SERVER 2012/X64 64BIT
WINDOWS SRV 2008 R2/X64 64BIT
WINDOWS SRV 2012 R2/X64 64BIT
WINDOWS SERVER 2016 LTSB 64BIT
WINDOWS SERVER 2019 LTSC 64BIT
WINDOWS SERVER 2022 64BIT

entry.title: Re: PCA Role in 2025
entry.content:
Retirement of Partner Cloud Architect role Effective 1st Jan 2025, all NEW indirect opportunities will be processed by SAP Enterprise Cloud Services Cloud Architect Advisor team. Partner Cloud Architects are no longer required to submit TVRF's for review. Please request the industry Account Executives/ Midmarket Sales Executive to raise a Cloud Architect Advisor request internally. All existing opportunities handled by Partner Cloud Architects which have slipped into Q1 2025 can be RESUBMITTED by Partner Cloud Architects UNTIL 31st Jan 2025. The RESUBMISSION link will be kept active until then and the Technical Viability Review Form (TVRF) review process will be supported.Partner Cloud Architects should use the RESUBMISSION link to ensure continuity of their TVRF reviews. No new submissions will be accepted. Any deal that goes beyond 31st Jan 2025 will be taken over by the SAP Cloud Architect AdvisorTeam. A handover MUST be performed by the Partner Cloud Architect to the SAP Cloud Architect Advisor for such deals. Please note that ALL deals that have been processed/will be processed (until 31st Jan 2025) under the Partner Cloud Architect model needs to be taken through to successful Sales to Delivery handover. SAP will not take over such deals. The previously qualified Partner Cloud Architects are encouraged to engage with customers on the broad presales activities including solution architecture, RISE with SAP methodology and clean core considerations.

Good news is that you can still talk about clean core 😶

entry.title: SAP Developer News, January 30th, 2025
entry.content:
Description

What’s New in SAP Build: Q4 2024 Webinar

  • Webinar Recordings (for Public) https://workzone.one.int.sap/site#workzone-home&/groups/zJfVxkPC5b2kQfs6YahqyX/content?folder_id=TfxCd7kH9hdfZ1hdvxFyBU&view_mode=list
  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/what-s-new-in-sap-build-q4-2024-edition/ba-p/13980162

SAP Build Code – What’s New January 2025

  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – January 2025 Edition: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/product-updates-for-sap-build-code-january-2025-edition/ba-p/13993021
  • Deprecation of Java Tools in SAP Web IDE: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/deprecation-of-java-tools-in-sap-web-ide/ba-p/13997766
  • SAP Build Code Joule Evolves: Unveiling Multi-Agent Support for Advanced CAP Application Generation: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-code-joule-evolves-unveiling-multi-agent-support-for-advanced-cap/ba-p/13995395

Upcoming CAP January Release 2025

  • npm @SAP/cds-dk https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap/cds-dk
  • CAP Release page January 2025 | capire

SAP Community active on Bluesky social media

  • SAP Community Account on Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/sapcommunity.bsky.social
  • Starter Pack SAP Community:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lfnowo7ykb2l
  • Starter Pack SAP Developer Advocates:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lgbebsq43a2x
  • Starter Pack SAP Champions:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lgbesf6hqa2r
  • Starter Pack SAP Mentors:  https://bsky.app/starter-pack/sapcommunity.bsky.social/3lgbem6f4u32p

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 What’s New in SAP Build: Q4 2024 Webinar

2:13 SAP Build Code What’s New Jan 2025

5:11 Upcoming CAP January Release 2025

6:14 SAP Community active on Bluesky social media

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro]This is the SAP Developer News for January 30th, 2025.

[Shrini] Hello SAP developers. I hope many of you got a chance to watch the webinar yesterday on the new SAP Build features released in Q4 2024. There were updates on product-specific highlights on tools such as SAP Build Process Automation, build code, build apps, and WorkZone with some cool demos on the new features. Here are some of my favorites. The design time UI in build apps has been redesigned allowing for a more intuitive user experience. Different pages in an app are easily accessible via drop down. Less frequently used tasks are positioned under app settings. Preview and publish options are now more prominent. In the process automation, now you can create and manage process variants. Process variants allow a variant template developer to create processes with pre-configured settings and a variant management user, usually a business configuration expert can modify the template variant to suit his needs which changes like change or order of variance, modify the start condition, release and deploy variants, etc. In WorkZone standard edition, the integration of Joule is a game changer. Joule in WorkZone provides answers and insights from any connected system via prompts, eliminating the need to switch between different tools and user interfaces. Note that this feature is not available in the free trial. Joule integrated in SAP Build Code, now it's seamless migration of SAP UI5 applications from JavaScript to TypeScript. SAP Build Code has an adaptation editor tool which is a new tool for extending SAP UI5 applications. It replaces the SAP UI5 Visual Editor. There are many more features. release in the last quarter, do check out the webinar recording and the blog post explaining all the features, the link to which are in the description below.

[Thomas] My colleague, Shri has already brought you some of the news about SAP build enhancements that were released in January of this year. I want to do a little breakout there on specific enhancements for SAP build code. Of course, build code is part of SAP build build and a little bit. bit of the news was covered in his segment, but for professional developers, I want to give you a little additional information. First of all, in SAP Build Code, we see the new project overview. This is to really replace the project Explorer and give you a better visualization of your project and in particular match up the structure to what you're seeing when you're using the storyboard editor. Next, we see improvements when working with remote function calls. There we've added the ability for the run configuration to be updated automatically as well as support both basic and principle propagation. Next, we see the inline editing for CDS entities. This allows you to edit entities and their attributes without having to switch between the graphical editor and the text or code-based editor. And we see the deprecation of Java tools for SAP WebIDE Now, this is an important note because this has to do with the deprecation of the SAP Java BuildPack version 1. Now, that deprecation is going to take place in June of 2025, and at that time, the older tools in the SAP Web IDE for Java development will also be deprecated. Now, this just means that you need to be using the SAP Business Application Studio, which we recommend that you use anyway. So by June 2025, please plan on moving your CAP Java and other Java-based, uh, tooling-based projects to the business application studio. And finally, the last item has to do with the Joule code generative, uh, capabilities in SAP build code. We see the agent, uh, are the, Joule assistant going multi-agent with the latest enhancements, particularly we see this in the CAP, new project generation. And now what you can do is you can give it a robust description of what you want in your entire CAP application to do, and in a single prompt, it will respond by both generating the application, generating sample data, and generating application logic for what you described. This used to be several different calls and interactions that you'd have to have with the Joule assistant and now, with it being multi-agent, you can do that in all one prompt. So look for all these enhancements in SAP Build Code. And of course, the blog post are all in the links to the show note of this episode.

[Kevin] The January 2025 cap releases right around the corner and we got some great release notes for you. The new CDS parser replacing ANTLR is almost done. With the new parser, you will get reduced installations and faster parsing, as well as improved code completion. And with the new release, you will also be able to use enum symbols when defining a default value. More enum symbols support will come in the future. And on the Java side, you can now use predicates as select items to evaluate Boolean expressions on the database. Odata v401 allows you to invoke functions using implicit parameter aliases, and this invocation style is now also supported by the CAP Java runtime. And CDS debug has been extended for Java, so you can easily debug local and remote Java applications. And lastly, I want to mention that IntelliJ has now SAP CDS language support. There are even more great news about the upcoming release, and you can read all about them in detail on the CAP release page.

[Josh] Hey there, it's Mr. Bluebeard, and I hope you are enjoying the blue sky. It is very windy and cold. outside today, so I came inside to talk to you about Blue Sky, not that Blue Sky, but about the social media site, Blue Sky. SAP is getting more and more active on Blue Sky. We've got the SAP community there. They've had a great feature called starter packs. So if you don't want to follow just the SAP community site, but also a bunch of individuals within other groups, you can quickly grab a starter pack for something like the SAP mentors, champions, developer advocates, or you can just continue to follow individuals. I've got an account there along with a bunch of other people that are inside of those other groups. It's just a great place where information can go and live freely. It was started by some people that started at different social media site years ago. And this is just basically a reboot of a place to go for short, micro -blogging, links, pictures, videos to things you care about. So follow accounts like the SAP community or maybe the UI5 group if you're into that. So there are more and more groups popping up every day. And Blue Sky is not just a song by a great band called ELO Right, DJ? It's a great place to go and share information. We'll see you there. Bye.

entry.title: Re: Combining Multiple Wildcard Searches in Same Report
entry.content: Thanks drs_macgyver. I edited my orginal post and included a new screenshot. I was not able to paste my screenshot in the 'Comment' box. Much appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Fiori Tools VSCode extension activates in more than 150 secondes 😲
entry.content:
Hi @sebtalasi,

Thanks for the feedback.

We are looking into some more improvements over the next months to hopefully improve this further. 

Regards,

Austin

entry.title: How to change the year in number ranges with nr status
entry.content: I am trying to change the year using Tcode: fbn1 but the system does not recognize it. How to overcome this problem.

entry.title: Vendor Novation in SAP S/4HANA Public Sector Management
entry.content:
Dear Experts, I am looking for information on the SAP Best Practice for supporting a vendor novation in the S/4HANA Public Sector environment. With SAP SRM sundowning in 2027, when a customer is using S/4HANA and Public Sector Management, what is the best practice for handling vendor novations when there are existing contracts and purchase orders in place, specifically when the commitments cover multiple years. For example, a contract and related purchase orders are created in a given fiscal year, let's assume FY23 with Vendor A, and then in FY24, Vendor A is acquired and merged with Vendor B. How should the public sector customer handle this novation? How should the existing contract with Vendor A and now Vendor B be updated? How would the associated purchase orders under this contract be updated for Vendor B while still reflecting the budgeting as applied in FY23?

Is there a best practice for S/4HANA to support closing the previous purchase orders and opening new purchase orders that still reflect the budget period of FY23 but using the new Vendor B that can be reflected against the updated contract with Vendor B that was originally with Vendor A?

Could this be accomplished using the Financial Management (FM) Multi-Year Budget Execution or is there a different or better option for handling the vendor novation?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Wrap up and Winner announcement . did I miss it?
entry.content: I'm afraid we haven't yet been able to publish the winner details as we are still in the process of legal validation of all the winners.  Just one left to go, so hopefully we are close to being able to share that info.

entry.title: SAP EWM-YARD Management
entry.content:
Introduction: In this Blog we will learn about the Relationship between Door location group – Storage type location group.

Configuration to maintain Door to Storage type relationship:-

As per the SAP help for ‘Define Location group relationship’, Yard can be configured for auto selection of Door based on the Door Location group and Storage type Location Group. Screenshot as below:-

 

In ECC Sandbox, following setting are made to test the functionality:-

  1. Define Yard Location:-

Path - SAP Customizing Implementation Guide ->Logistics Execution->Yard Management->Yard Map-> Define Yard Locations.

 

 

  1. Define Door:-

Path:-SAP Customizing Implementation Guide ->Logistics Execution->Yard Management->Yard Map-> Define Doors.

Maintained the Location groups & Storage types for the Door separately.

 

 

  1. Assign Storage Types to Yard Location Groups:-

Path: - SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Logistics Execution->Yard Management->Yard Map->Assign Storage Types to Yard Location Groups

Maintained respective Location groups for storage types.

 

 

  1. Define Location Group Relationships

Maintain relationship between the Door Location Group and Related Location group.

 

  1. Material Master:-

Maintained two materials for the two separate storage types 101 and 201 with fixed bins maintained.

  • For Part Number 988 –

Storage type search indicator – 101

Fixed bin – 01-01-04.

  • For Part Number 989 –

Storage type search indicator – 102

Fixed bin – 01-01-01

 

 

 

 

 

 

Storage type search sequence:-

 

  1. Creation of two separate inbound deliveries for the above mentioned Material numbers.

Two Inbound Deliveries created:-

IBD – 180001993 for part no 988

IBD – 180001994 for part no 989

Both assigned to separate vehicles and checked in.

When scheduled for Door movement, both the vehicles got scheduled to the respective doorsreferring to their Storage types.

Conclusion:

This blog post walks you through various steps involved in Yard Management with test results.

Thanks

Munaff .J

entry.title: Re: BADI_FQM_FLOW_ADJUST_CORE not get triggering from MM ME22N in HNANA
entry.content: Hi alex. Which BADI works for MM and FI Flows?

entry.title: The variable on/off checkbox is disabled for customer field (KNDNR) for the COPA in KE31
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to configure a COPA report in KE31, but I noticed that the "Variable On/Off" checkbox is disabled for the Customer field (KNDNR).

I couldn't find KNDNR as a characteristic in KEA5 or KEA0, which makes me wonder if it's a predefined field that cannot be changed.

Additionally, I have already created a global variant in KE3E for the KNDNR field, but the checkbox remains disabled.

Is there a way to enable the "Variable On/Off" checkbox for this characteristic? Or would I need to create a custom characteristic for the customer field to achieve this functionality?

Any insights or SAP Notes related to this would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Issue with HANA Cloud Instance in BTP
entry.content: I hope you filed a Case via SAP Support.

entry.title: I'm following the SAP Enable Now Learning Journey and no credential details in the exercise section
entry.content:
Hello.
I'm following the SAP Enable Now Learning Journey and for the exercises section I cannot find the information of what System should I use, what credentials or the "Manager" address.

For your reference, for example, "Exercise 1: Recording Preparation" includes a PDF with the step by step, but no info of the Practice System or credentials. It also indicates to access Google Chrome to the manager, but no link nor credentials were provided. "Exercise 2: Recording" also includes a PDF but no info. It is the same for all the exercises.

Can you please help indicating where this info can be found?

Thanks

Giovanni

entry.title: Re: S4H Fiori Launchpad Message - Password login no longe...
entry.content: It could also be a security feature. I suggest to clarify via SAP Support.

it's been a while, but how are the plans going for this sample content? The last update was in 2023. Is the content now compliant with IAS 12 Income tax, as well as with IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements?

Thanks for answering. 🙂

entry.title: Re: RFC destination not showing up in destinations in Bui...
entry.content: Hi Dan, so that means that RFC isn't supported in Build Apps or?

entry.title: pay range in position
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to configure pay ranges on position by following below instructions as mentioned in this

KBA - 2458839 - How to Configure Pay Range in Positions - Employee Central - SAP for Me

how ever I see pay range is displayed on the fly before entering the values at position, but I do not see pay range getting displayed once after position has saved.

I have configured the business rule as shown below

I have saved the rule under manage data - object configuration as shown below

I have saved the rule also under configure object definitions - position - company field as shown below

with this configuration steps in place, now that I am trying to either create a new position or modify an existing position under manage data tool. as you can see pay range is also getting displayed

but once the position creation is done, the value at pay range disappears unfortunately.

Any reason what was missing here?

entry.title: Re: Destination usage in SAP BAS.
entry.content:
I would suggest you use the SAP Cloud SDK HTTP Client:

https://sap.github.io/cloud-sdk/docs/js/features/connectivity/http-client

as it will support the resolution of destinations out of the box. In BAS you can set the destination in a destinations environment variable and use http://<dest-name>.dest in the URL

entry.title: Re: PowerBi x Sap Enable Now - Lesson Detail Report for a...
entry.content:
Hi Laura,

I think that the report that you are trying to run can only be run for a single Simulation at a time; I can get that report working for a single Simulation.  You will need to try one of the other reports to be able to report on all Simulations in a WA.  I would start with the Raw Record Report and see if that provides you with the information that you are after.

 

 

 

 

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Jwt issuer is not configured
entry.content: Is your issue resolved? We started facing this error in one of our live applications. Looking for a solution

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori app to find out transactions for apps
entry.content: I think you should try https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/

entry.title: Re: Managed RAP Remote Service Based CAPM App: Gateway HT...
entry.content: I would think you should file this as a SAP Support Case when you're using the current CAP version.

entry.title: Re: Aggregate the data respective field in Signal
entry.content:
Good day Shravanr,

Thank you for sharing your question on the SAP Community!  

It is recommended that you create an SAP for Me case in order to receive further assistance.  The instructions to do so are as follows:  

Please create a support case using SAP for Me (log in with S-User ID and password). 
You will need to select the specific SAP Signavio product / component: 
 
System: 
Search for SAP Signavio  
 
Product Area: 
SAP Signavio Process Manager > SAP Signavio Process Manager 
 
Component: 
BPI (Business Process Intelligence) 
---  
BPI-PI (SAP Signavio Process Insights) or 
BPI-SIG (SAP Business Transformation Suite by SAP Signavio) – Product & Topic 
 
You can find more details on creating an SAP For Me Case, please review the SAP Signavio Onboarding Resource Center.  Thank you.
 
Kind regards,
 
KantriceD

entry.title: Re: Wrap up and Winner announcement . did I miss it?
entry.content: crossing fingers, LOL

entry.title: Re: ZOOM VLS integration with SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content: Instructors cannot start zoom recording for zoom sessions created from LMS.  Is this not possible or do we have to do something to enable zoom recording?

entry.title: MDG CE Groupings and Number ranges
entry.content:
Dear experts

I have read in the MDG Cloud Edition documentation, that the groupings and the number ranges are fixed and cannot be changed. I wanted to know how does it work in the case of the replication towards S4 HANA. Is it possible to transcodificate the original grouping from MDG-CE into the custom ones which are in S4 HANA, for example using a middleware and a custom field in MDG to store this information?

Will MDG be still able to recognise the key mapping of the record? 

Did anyone ever experienced this problem?

 

Thank you very much

best regards

entry.title: Re: ZOOM VLS integration with SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content: Also I would like to know if during zoom sessions we use breakout rooms, does it not automatically record zoom attendance?  I think someone asked this before but we did not get a response.  Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Step by Step Implementation of Secure Login Service for SAP GUI - BTP SSO
entry.content: Does this configuration require the backend SAP ABAP host have access to the Active Domain Controller to validate the USERS Kerberos Token?  Specifically, I'm asking in a SAP RISE scenario where the SAP ABAP Host does not have access to the customer's AD,

entry.title: Requirement of 4 savings plan and employer contribution cap
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a requirement where we have 4 savings plan - RRSP , TFSA and two others for the employees in Canada. Employee contribution and employer contributions for these saving plans are percentages of annual salary and editable by the employee. The Employer matches double of the employee contribution subject to a maximum of 10% across all the 4 savings plans. Since both the employee and employer contributions have to be replicated to EC payroll and the country is non USA, savings plan (which is for USA only) and other pension plans were ruled out and the only option we had to implement this was Workplace Pension Plan. Workplace Pension Plan does not allow multiple pension funds and allows single pension fund only and therefore we had to create 4 Benefits of type Pension / Retirement Savings Plan, one for RRSP, one for TFSA, and so on for the other two. When we are saving 1 of those 4 benefits we are not able to get the employer contributions employee has saved in other 3 and implement the 10 % employer contribution cap through a business rule. Any inputs on how we can implement this requirement of 4 savings plan and meet the 10 % employer contribution cap.

Thanks a lot for your help.
Regards,
Rahul

entry.title: Re: Control Editability of benefits based on the benefit...
entry.content: Thansk this helped us meet our requirement.

entry.title: Direct URL for SAP HANA Incentive Management with SSO
entry.content:
We are using SAP HANA for Incentive Management, and the application is integrated with our company's SSO. As an administrator, I need to find the direct URL that bypasses SSO authentication.

Where can I locate this URL in the configuration? This will help provide access to an implementation partner who needs to work on the application.

Appreciate any guidance!

entry.title: Re: Migrate data with CDS view behaviour in migration coc...
entry.content: The CDS object behavior I believe is in reference to creating a custom data migration object, when you get to the Target Structure, it provides an option to choose (CDS) Behavior Definition, looking for documentation or guidance on how that works. LTMOM > Create Custom Data Migration Object > Target Structure > CDS Behavior

Could you please explain me the details about how to obtain it?
Thank you so much!!!

entry.title: Re: How to reset SAP* password..
entry.content:
Hi Junaid,

I recently had the same issue when I forgot my password for ES5, there is a link on the log pad but if the link is not visible then scroll down the section where it says "Welocme to ICC Test system....." which took me sometime to figure out. Here it will display the below link. Follow the below link to reset the password:

For user creation/password reset:

https://register.sapdevcenter.com/SUPSignForms/

Hope this will help!

HK

entry.title: Re: Purchasing a discounted certification attempt
entry.content:
The voucher would be for the examen CERS01.

Thank you so much!

entry.title: Re: IAS application:Subject Name Identifier
entry.content:
Hello,

The Subject Name identifier is an attribute that Identity Authentication sends to the application as name ID in the SAML 2.0 assertions

The source is from where this attribute come:

  • Identity Directory - This is the local user attribute.
  • Corporate IdP - Eg: Azure

For more information you can check the SAP HELP below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-subject-name-identifier-sent-to-application?q=subject+name

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL trigger in a CAP project
entry.content: Is there a specific reason you want to use a trigger and not use the service handlers which accomplish the same?

entry.title: OData Exploration with EPM Tutorial - ES5
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am self-learning for OData Exploration with EPM Tutorial, I have logged in to "SAP Gateway Demo system via the Web GUI" successfully, then need to enter transaction SICF but on the Fiori screen the Command Window is NOT visible and I can't figure out how to make this visible. In the tutorial it says:

If necessary, use the arrow button to make the OK Code field appear, so you can enter transaction codes.

Any help, 

Thanks

HK

entry.title: Re: Mapping Rules in Cloud Foundary
entry.content:
Complementing the previous answer.

About creating a default group and assigning users to it:
The owners of the Custom Identity Provider are responsible for explaining how to create groups and assign the user to these groups.

If you are using SAP Identity Authentication as Custom Identity Provider, you can refer to:

  • If you need to create a new group, refer to: Create new user group
  • If the group is already created, but you need to assign users to it, refer to:
    • Add users to group
    • Assign Groups to a User

All the best,
Vini
SAP Support

entry.title: TCode: /SCWM/QRSETUP (Argument: Inspection Type Detailed Data) screen is missing
entry.content:
Issue Description:  (Argument: Inspection Type Detailed Data) screen is missing

While accessing TCode /SCWM/QRSETUP, the detailed screen for entering key quality inspection parameters is missing. The issue details are as follows: 

  • Missing Fields/Sections:
    • Inspection Type (Field not visible or not editable)
    • Task List (Not appearing in the setup screen)
    • Group and Counter (Unable to enter or select values)
  • Observed Behavior:
    • The system either skips the expected screen or does not display the required input fields.
    • Users are unable to configure quality rules correctly due to missing sections.
  • Expected Behavior:
    • The screen should allow input for Inspection Type, Task List, Group, and Counter as per standard SAP EWM Quality Rule Setup.
  • Error Message (if any):
    • Inspection lots not generated
  • Screenshots:
    • Attach relevant screenshots showing the missing sections.

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Log in to SAP EWM.
  2. Navigate to TCode /SCWM/QRSETUP.
  3. Attempt to create or modify a Quality Rule.
  4. The screen does not provide fields for Inspection Type, Task List, Group, and Counter.

Impact Analysis:

  • Does this affect warehouse quality inspection processes? ✅ Yes
  • Is there a workaround available? ❌ No
  • Is this affecting multiple users or a specific role? ✅ Multiple users in Quality Inspection Setup

Troubleshooting Performed:

  • Verified user authorization for /SCWM/QRSETUP.
  • Checked SAP Configuration (Quality Inspection and Task List Settings)

Requested Action:

  • Investigate missing screen sections and provide a resolution.
  • Check SAP Notes or system configuration affecting /SCWM/QRSETUP.
  • Provide a temporary workaround if an immediate fix is unavailable.

entry.title: Re: Unable to do PGI for Sales Order
entry.content: Hi, Please can you let me know how you resolved as I am having same issue. Please help. Thank you!

entry.title: Upcoming Webinar: The Future of AI for Talent Management
entry.content:
As the world of work continues to evolve, organizations must rethink how they attract, develop, and retain talent. With AI and talent intelligence playing an increasingly critical role, HR leaders need to embrace innovative strategies to stay ahead. Join us for an engaging webinar where we’ll explore how AI-driven innovations and skills-based talent strategies can help your organization unlock workforce potential and drive business success.

Join us on Tuesday, March 4th at 11:00 AM EST to discover how using an AI-enabled talent management system enables organizations to hire the best talent, create targeted learning pathways, optimize performance, and deliver personalized development opportunities at scale.

Key topics in this webinar include:

  • The latest HR trends and research insights on how AI is transforming talent management.
  • SAP’s AI strategy and how it supports accelerated growth, employee productivity, and skill development.
  • Live demo showcasing AI-driven innovations for attracting, retaining, and developing top talent.

Don’t miss this opportunity to see how AI can reshape your talent management strategy and empower your workforce to adapt, grow, and thrive.

Register now!

entry.title: Re: I have issues integrating Crystal for visual studio s...
entry.content:
The highest supported level in .NET 4.81 ("classic"). No support for .NET core.
If you search (Crystal Reports .NET Core), you'll find similar questions and answers.

entry.title: Re: Canada ECP- Cross-year retro payroll and tax for Cana...
entry.content:
@miteshjain- Were you able to resolve the issue?

We have similar issue with cross-year payroll. I saw in your comments that you were able to bring the earnings forward from prior year.

Your comment - "Standard calculation for Canada payroll has an option to bring forward the differences from prior year but the earnings are treated as non-periodic income and negative values are not taxed. Even if I change to regular taxable income and add to current pay period income (PP01/2025) to calculate the correct taxes".

Please let me know what has to be done to get the prior year earnings to current year period and to make it to regular taxable income. Appreciate your help.

entry.title: Re: Cross-year retro payroll and tax for Canada Payroll
entry.content:
@miteshjain- Were you able to resolve the issue?

We have similar issue with cross-year payroll. I saw in your comments on the other thread that you were able to bring the earnings forward from prior year.

Please let me know what has to be done to get the prior year earnings to current year period and to make it to regular taxable income. Appreciate your help.

entry.title: Re: Mass change hierarchy node in Profit Center master data
entry.content:
Hello Anais. Kindly review the options below. 

1. You can use the "Manage Global Hierarchies" app to make adjustments to the hierarchy nodes under Profit Centers. Here you can Select the Profit Center Hierarchy,  Once inside the app, you can select the hierarchy relevant to your Profit Centers. You will  see options to create, edit, and delete hierarchy nodes.

You can perform Mass Changes ,  mass change functionalities available in the app to update the nodes as required. This could include changing node names, assignment of profit centers, and other hierarchical attributes. You can use Spreadsheets (if applicable).  For large-scale changes, you might have the option to export the hierarchy to a spreadsheet, make changes, and then import it back. This feature, if available, allows easier mass maintenance. Once you are done please save and activate changes  after making the necessary changes, ensure that you save and activate these changes to reflect them in your system accordingly. Learn more with SAP Note 2953045 - How to create/import Cost Profit Center hierarchy with app Manage Global Accounting Hierarchies.

You can do the data upload through Excel files, which can be particularly useful for mass updates. Also you can download the appropriate template for profit center hierarchies. This template can usually be found in the ‘Import Data’ section or similar.  You can enter the new hierarchy data into the template Excel file. Please follow the template format strictly to avoid upload errors. In the ‘Import Data’ section of the relevant Fiori app, upload the completed Excel file.


2. In addition you can review Flexible hierarchies such as  profit center hierarchies that can be flexibly maintained in the Manage Flexible Hierarchies app. 

You can create and change hierarchies for profit center reporting. You can customize these hierarchies to fit your reporting needs and adapt them to your business requirements. Learn more about Mass update existing hierarchies based on changes to master data attributes. Learn more with implementation details here 

3. Additionally if you want to edit profit center master data then use Manage Profit Centers (Version 2). Select the profit centers you want to change by checking the boxes next to them. Click on the 'Edit' button to enter the mass edit mode. Once in edit mode, look for the hierarchy-related fields that need to be updated.

4. Lastly you can review SAP Build Process Automation to fully automate using the event mesh driven architecture. While you are in SAP S/4HANA Cloud , you can create a event that will trigger automatically the bot for uploading the hierarchy using upload template. You can learn more with documentation on Extensibility wizard and video. 

In addition you can review if you want to automate the screen navigation steps using the bot by recording web Fiori Application

Hope this helps

Thanks

entry.title: Re: FBL*H transactions - OOM Dump(STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT_FROM_GLOBAL_CONFIG) and analysis
entry.content:
Hi @raymond_giuseppi, 

I did an SQL trace but couldn't catch. How should I trace the scenario?

Scenario is; The SQL conditions are the same, just the query time is different!!

Kind regards, aatan.

entry.title: Re: How to avoid hard coding for if condition.
entry.content:
Hi @Praveen_Sankaraddi 

Its not a standard solution but a suggestion which is flexible for future changes in condition checking as well...

You may have a custom table which may have multiple Key Fields to identify records. Ex:

You may have warehouse no or Plant as 1 key and WRICEF no as another key and Functionality name (chkSalesOrg) column. and You maintain values. If you maintain values like this you have flexibility to maintain another sales org as second line with same Warehouse No, Plant.

Before checking any condition with hardcoded values you read this table values and match with your data and make decision.

Hope this helps...

Thanks-

entry.title: Re: Feature Control in RAP, Dynamic Feature Control, Part - 2
entry.content:
@Krishna_karale thanks for sharing...

Thanks-

entry.title: Re: FBL*H transactions - OOM Dump(STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT_FROM_GLOBAL_CONFIG) and analysis

"with less memory available, the SQL optimiser may choose different data access paths" 

Is there a way to prevent this? e.g. Can I use a hint? **NO_USE_HEX_PLAN etc.

Kind regards, aatan.

We want to use the Consumer Proxy to connect to the external SOAP Service.

When setting up the logical port in SOAManager, there is no option to use API Key.

Is there a way to connect to a SOAP service from S4 HANA using Consumer Proxy and using API key for authentication.

Open Job Application template -> Change something in the field mentioned in the error (such as adding a description) -> Publish again.

But still not working for us.

entry.title: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
Accelerating innovation while future-proofing your business

(Note: This is a fast-moving project so please bookmark this blog as we will keep updating the information as it becomes available)

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) is the gold standard for enterprise and global payroll with localization in 57 countries (52 by SAP, 5 by partners) and live productive usage in 84 (and counting). With more than 2000 companies around the globe, it is delivering more than 200 million pay statements per year.

ECP is SAP’s go-to, long-term payroll solution fueled by significant ongoing investment. Over last few months, we have embarked on a journey to revamp your favorite payroll product.  We are converting ECP to a modern technology foundation which will unlock new capabilities and elevate its performance for decades to come. We are working hard so these innovations reach our global customers quickly

Why is SAP embarking on this initiative?

The current technologies used for ECP are going out of maintenance by end of 2027. This has opened an opportunity for us to leverage the powerful capabilities of the new technology foundation as well as new tools to improve on our service delivery while enabling you to future-proof your business for years to come.

What are the benefits to our customers?

Among the key capabilities and innovations our customers will be able to experience through this technology modernization are:

  • Rollout of new features to meet customer demand
    • Position Budgeting Control (PBC) for regulated industries (e.g. public sector, healthcare, higher education)
    • Business AI capabilities to drive HR operations efficiency (e.g. Explain my pay statement)
    • Updated HORIZON UI for a unified user experience (consistent with the rest of SAP SuccessFactors HCM)
    • Enhanced Payroll Control Center capabilities to better manage payroll activities

  • Operational  enhancements
    • High availability set-up for production systems
    • Ability to get enhanced encryption(planed)

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

  1. Why is SAP doing this?
    • The current technologies used for ECP are going out of maintenance by end of 2027 which has opened an opportunity for us to leverage the powerful capabilities of the SAP HANA database and associated technologies.

  2. What are the current technologies used for ECP? What modern technology foundation are you moving to?
    • Current technologies: SAP ECC 6.08, SAP NetWeaver 7.40 and SAP MaxDB database
    • Modern technologies: SAP S/4HANA 2023, ABAP Platform 2023, SAP HANA database

  3. When will the modernized ECP available for me? I need to know when to request budget, and resources for my own planning
    • New Customers: At the time of publication of this blog (Jan 31, 2025), all our new customers in USA, Australia and Europe are being provisioned EC Payroll on modern technology foundation. Rest of the world will follow over coming months.
    • Existing Customers: Conversion planning is in full swing. It will be a phased approach which will start in the second quarter of 2025 and will continue throughout 2027. We will reach out to customers and partners on the details of the conversion planning.

  4. Where can I find documentation how to use the new technology foundation?
    • The standard ECP documentation has been enhanced with chapters targeting the new technology foundation and is located here

  5. How long will the conversion last, when is the latest date that I will be converted?
    • We will start in second quarter 2025 with pilot customers and targeting to complete it by the end of 2027

  6. How should we plan for the conversion to modernized SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll?
    • We will publish the preliminary version of a detailed conversion guide by End-March.

  7. Will the Business AI capabilities like Explain my pay statement functionality be offered only on the modernized ECP or will it be available for current ECP version also?
    • All the new innovations and enhancements will be available ONLY on ECP built on the new technology foundation.

  8. Will the Business AI capabilities like Explain my pay statement functionality be offered on Private Cloud Edition (PCE)?
    • No. They will ONLY be offered on SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll on the modern technology foundation

  9. Who do I contact for questions?
    • Please visit this blog as this will be kept updated as more and more details become available. In the meantime, please contact your SAP account executive or partner for any questions.

entry.title: Migrating roles from SAC to Datasphere for row level security
entry.content:
Your organization has been using SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) for a significant period, where you implemented row-level security by defining roles in SAC and applying additional filters to the models. The security framework in SAC was functioning seamlessly. However, your organization has now decided to transition to SAP Datasphere. In SAP Datasphere, row-level security is implemented using permission tables and "Data Access Controls" (DACs).

There are many blogs about how DataSphere security works. This blog explores effective methods to simplify the management of users, roles via creation of a role configuration table to streamline updates in the permission tables. It provides a practical approach to managing security in SAP Datasphere analytical models. It assumes that you have somewhat implemented row-level security in Datasphere and are looking to effectively manage users/roles in easier way. This blog introduces a straightforward solution to replicate and enhance the security mechanisms previously used in SAC within the Datasphere environment.

SAP Datasphere implements row-level security using permission tables and data access controls (DAC), which works seamlessly when there is a single permission table for all users. However, if your requirement is complex, managing multiple permission tables can quickly become a maintenance challenge, as administrators must ensure that entries for each user exist in every permission table.

In this blog, we will utilize the DataSphere Sample Sales Fact dataset to demonstrate how to implement security on two specific columns: Product Category ID from the Product Dimension and SalesOrg in the Fact Table. The implementation will leverage two permission tables while maintaining a flexible design that can scale to support security across multiple tables. The main objective is to illustrate how entries in multiple permission tables can easily managed, by dynamically populating tables through a central permission configuration table and a user-to-role mapping table.

After implementing the necessary security measures, this is how column-based security appears.

Now, let's examine the two methods for adding data to the permission table:

1. First Method:
This method requires entering details for each user in each permission table (if you have complex security requirements). As the permission administrator adds data, they must ensure that accurate entries are added into the permission tables. If modifications are needed later, the process can become complex.

2. Second Method:
This method involves using an Users table and a Role Configuration table to load data into the permission table with the help of data flows:

- Users Table:
The structure of the Users table is designed to store the user IDs of all users who have access to SAC, along with the roles they require. The permission administrator only needs to add entries for new users in this table.

- Role Configuration Table:
The Role Configuration table defines details for each role, including criteria, logic, and information for the first and second columns. This setup is done once for each role.

- Data Flow and Task Chains:
To load data into each permission table, task chains and data flows are used:
- Data flows join the data from the Users table and the Role Configuration table via a view, then load the results into the permission table.
Here is the SQL code used for the view 

SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY PermissionTableName ORDER BY UserName DESC) AS Permission_ID, 
"UserName" AS User_ID, "Restriction", "Criterion", "Operator",
"First_Value", "Second_Value" FROM "Users" U
JOIN "RoleConfig" R ON U.PRDCT_CTGRY_Roles = R.RoleName
WHERE PermissionTableName = 'PERMISSION_PRDCT_CTGRY'

- Separate data flows are created to load the data in each permission table by running above SQL.

- Task chains link all the data flows together, so when a new user is added to the Users table, the administrator can run the task chain to update the permission table.

This design simplifies the job of the permission administrator and ensures all permissions are maintained in an organized and easily manageable structure.

entry.title: Adding tenant specific custom code using SaaS Extensibility
entry.content:
Hello SAP CAP Team,

the Cookbook for SaaS Extensibility describes the Extensibility options for additional data fields and UI Annotations using CDS. But is there also an option for tenant specific custom code?

Similar but yet unanswered questions were posted here: 

  1. Enhancing standard SAP code in CAP.
  2. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/adding-tenant-specific-custom-code-using-saas-extensibility/qaq-p/12374362 

Best regards
Venkatesh Hulekal

entry.title: What's new: SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain – 2502
entry.content:
Welcome to the latest release highlights for SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) for Supply Chain. In this blog, we will showcase the key enhancements and updates available with release 2502.  

Enhancements in the SAPIBP1 Sample Planning Area:

Let's start with enhancements added to the SAPIBP1 sample planning area.

  • Inventory Optimization now incorporates recency weighting in forecast error calculations, enhancing the statistical relevance of recent periods. This enables businesses to respond more quickly to changes in forecast error. To support this feature, two new output diagnostic key figures have been introduced to the planning area. These key figures are accessible in the Forecast Error Profiles app.

  • In the context of Inventory Optimization, we are streamlining the modeling process for the key figures: Production Lead Time IO Input from Upper Limit (UL) Delta and Transportation Lead Time IO Input from UL Delta. Instead of using a helper key figure approach, the original key figures are used directly in the calculation.
  • A new helper key figure has been introduced to support the unit of measure conversion for the Final Safety Stock (from IO)(Last Period Aggregation) key figure. This new helper key figure is essential for the calculation of  inventory components, utilizing either the manually adjustable final safety stock key figure or the recommended safety stock.

For more details on the different enhancements please check this presentation.

Maintenance, Repair, and Operations

The IBP Best Practices for Maintenance, Repair, and Operations (MRO) have been enhanced with a new preconfigured planner workspace and an analytics story. These preconfigured assets are designed to improve the existing solution process IBP for maintenance, repair, and operations (75C). The enhancements facilitate effective planning and analytics for comprehensive MRO scenarios and allow a fast adoption of best-in-class analytics and planning UIs. The available workbooks and story provide deep insights from high-level overviews to detailed part location information, allowing data-driven decision-making during the maintenance planning process.

The new Analytics Story includes visualizations on key process steps such as demand and segmentation analysis, inventory planning, supply and capacity review, and finance review. Below are some screenshots showcasing the sample analytic story, including the following pages:

  • Segmentation & Demand Analysis
  • Inventory Planning
  • Supply & Capacity Review
  • Financial Review

The new Planner Workspace pages cover the detailed level of all relevant input and output generated during the maintenance and operations planning  process. Here are some screenshots that offer a glimpse into the planner workspace. As you can see in the provided screenshots, different workbooks are available for the key process steps: 

  • Segmentation & Demand Analysis
  • Inventory Planning
  • Supply and Capacity Review
  • Financial Review

 

Demand Planning

In the solution process IBP for demand – new product introduction (5OZ) we have included an example that demonstrates how to display active product assignments within the SAP IBP, add-in for Microsoft Excel. This enhancement allows planners to verify existing product assignments directly, eliminating the need to switch to the Manage Product Lifecycle app.

 

Here some more useful links:

  • Highlight video for SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning - 2502 is available here.
  • SAP Signavio Process Navigator to download all assets of SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning.
  • In case you missed our last webinar about S/4 HANA Cloud, public edition integration and preconfigured analytics stories you can check out here the recording.
  • In case you want to learn more about SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain I recommend our blog series. The first blog is available here.

Thank you for reading our release highlights blog. Stay tuned for more updates and enhancements in future releases.

entry.title: Re: How to Add a Nested JSON Object in RestService.SendRequest Action Body in SAP MDK?
entry.content:
Thanks for your response! I am using the Send Request Action, and here is the metadata of the action:
{
"_Type": "Action.Type.RestService.SendRequest",
"ActionResult": {
"_Name": "rest_service"
},
"OnSuccess": "/restcall/Actions/CloseModalPage_Complete.action",
"Target": {
"Service": "/restcall/Services/api.service",
"Path": "/verification/v1/verifications",
"RequestProperties": {
"Method": "POST",
"Headers": {
"Content-Type": "application/json",
"Authorization": "Basic YzU4NTY3NGEtOTk4NC00ZmVjLWFjNmQtMTk2YTNkMjJlN2Y2Okx1NCtFK1R2ZFVPSUYvY2hMcWF4eWPQ==",
"Accept-Language": "en-US"
},
"Body": {
"identify": "/restcall/Rules/otp.js",
"method": "sms"
},
"FetchCSRF": false
}
}
}

 

The expected body format for  api is
{
"identity": {
"type": "number",
"endpoint": "+919490767699"
},
"method": "sms"
}

Since I wasn't sure how to pass this nested JSON directly in the action, I used a rule to generate the identity field:
export default function otp(clientAPI) { let identityValue = JSON.stringify({ type: "number", endpoint: "+919490767699" }); return identityValue; }

However, I think the structure may not be correct when the request is sent. How should I correctly pass this nested JSON in the Send Request Action?

Would appreciate any insights on this! Thanks



 

 
 



@bill_froelich

entry.title: Re: Issue getting DMS Folder Content in SAP Build Process...

Thanks for your reply. So, I added the destination under Control Tower -> Destination. This was missing. I also added another activity in the Automation "Select DMS", and gave the destination under its property. When I run the automation now, I do not get the Destination issue.

However I am getting another issue, "Response Code 405 (Method not allowed", I am using the standard DMS activity to get folder content  (well atleast the SAP help docs says so). 

Regards

Sandip

entry.title: Internal Reconciliation : Field cannot be updated (ODBC -1029) [Message 131-183]
entry.content:
Hello,
I'm experiencing an issue during Internal Reconciliation for a specific vendor. Despite having multiple invoices, journal entries, and receipts, some invoices fail to reconcile when I click the Reconcile button.

The error message I receive is: "Field cannot be updated (ODBC -1029) [Message 131-183]".

Notably, some invoices from the same vendor are reconciling successfully.

Please advise on how to resolve this issue.

entry.title: Re: Is there an option to verify the content in QATEXT upon transport release in STMS_QA?
entry.content:
Hi Bärbel, 

I know that you asked for something different. But still I want to stress out the checking before release:

If you check naming only before Import, the name cannot be change any more and therefor the whole transport has to be replaced creating New versions in the Dev system(s). If you block the Request upon release this cannot happen.

But I guess we should do so. Regards, Peter

entry.title: Re: Message No F5787

entry.title: How To Read Data From TCP/IP Socket and Port Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
Dear All ,

 I need to read Machine data using a TCP/IP Ethernet cable.

We have the following data which is going to be used for TCP/IP Ethernet

Local Host IP: 192.xx.xx.xx ( My PC IP address )

Remote Host IP: 192.xx.xx.xx ( Machine device IP address )

Remote Port: 1702

For Example, the Image is attached where the third-party utility is connected with my Remote Hot IP and Port and reads data I need to develop same functionality in SAP Can you help with this 

I also attached the SAP Version that I used.

entry.title: Re: Upcoming Webinar: HR and the Role of AI in 2025
entry.content: Interested in learning about enhancing your talent strategy with AI? Join our upcoming webinar, The Future of AI for Talent Management, on Tuesday, March 4th at 11:00AM EST.

entry.title: Re: Transport Organizer in Eclipse & ABAP Development Tools cannot create / edit transports?!
entry.content: Agreed. STMS should be on Eclipse. Going to SAP GUI even within the Eclipse is not great and leads me to use SAP GUI anyway.
